info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Schedule Appointments API
info.description: The Schedule Appointments API enables users to efficiently manage appointment bookings within the application. This endpoint allows for the creation of new appointments by capturing necessary details such as date, time, and participant information. Typical consumers of this API include scheduling applications and calendar management systems aiming to streamline the appointment setting process. By leveraging this API, developers can enhance user experience for clients needing reliable scheduling solutions.

post.description: This endpoint allows users to create a new appointment by providing the necessary details. Ensure that all required fields are filled out to successfully create the appointment.

requestBody.description: This request body is used to create a new appointment. It should include all necessary information to schedule the appointment, such as the date, time, client details, and any specific requirements. Ensure that the data adheres to the schema defined in the components section.

404.description: The requested resource could not be found.

502.description: The server received an invalid response from the upstream server.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

timestamp.description: The time when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the request was successful.

message.description: A human-readable message providing more details about the error.
message.example: Unable to process bank account creation request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The endpoint path that was being accessed.

trace.description: A string tracing the error through the application.

suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: The response schema for a 400 error in the appointment operations POST request.

example.message: Unable to process bank account creation request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database timeout occurred while saving account information.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.title: ErrorDetails

customer.description: Indicates whether the error is related to a customer issue.

errorCode.description: Specific error code representing the error type.

message.description: A message providing further information about the error.
message.example: The server encountered an unexpected condition.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.description: Schema representing additional details of the error encountered.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: AppointmentResponse

policyId.description: The unique identifier for the policy associated with the appointment.

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer related to the appointment.

policyType.description: The type of policy (e.g., health, auto, etc.) associated with the appointment.

coverageAmount.description: The total coverage amount provided by the policy.

premiumAmount.description: The premium amount that needs to be paid for the policy.

status.description: The current status of the appointment.

startDate.description: The start date of the policy validity.

endDate.description: The end date of the policy validity.

message.description: Additional message or information regarding the appointment.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Response object for a successful appointment operation that includes policy and customer details.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates the status of the response, where false denotes an error.

error.description: A brief description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message about the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for policy creation.

path.description: The path of the request that caused the error.

trace.description: A stack trace for debugging the error.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for the user on what to do next.
suggestion.example: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema for the 404 error response when an appointment operation fails.

example.message: Customer details not found for policy creation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Error Response for Appointment Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the status of the operation is successful or not.

error.description: A brief description of the error that occurred.

message.description: A detailed message describing the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for policy creation.

path.description: The path of the request that caused the error.

trace.description: A trace identifier for tracking the error.

suggestion.description: A suggestion on what the client can do to resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the error response structure returned when an appointment operation fails with a 502 status code.

example.message: Customer details not found for policy creation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: AppointmentRequestBody

customernumber.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

policyType.description: The type of policy being requested.

coverageAmount.description: The total coverage amount for the policy.

premiumAmount.description: The premium amount to be paid for the policy.

startDate.description: The start date of the policy in ISO 8601 format.

endDate.description: The end date of the policy in ISO 8601 format.

beneficiarycode.description: The code representing the beneficiary associated with the policy.

paymentmode.description: The mode of payment for the premium (e.g., monthly, yearly).

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the request body for creating an appointment, including customer details, policy information, coverage, and payment mode.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: ErrorResponse

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates the success status of the request.

message.description: A human-readable message providing details about the error.
message.example: Customer details not found for policy creation.

path.description: The API path that was requested when the error occurred.

trace.description: A stack trace or additional context for debugging purposes.

details.description: Additional details specific to the error encountered.

suggestion.description: Suggested actions or resolutions for the user.
suggestion.example: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the structure of an error response for the POST operation on appointments.

example.message: Customer details not found for policy creation.

details.message: No registered customer profile exists for the provided customer ID.

example.suggestion: Verify customer details and retry the policy creation request.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

content-type.description: The `Content-Type` header indicates the media type of the resource. In this case, it specifies that the response body format is JSON.
